description: Headline OWN-DECISION gate: the platform's enforcement determination on flagged content. Unlike the payer/screening/dispute SoR-seam verticals, NO external party renders this — the platform decides, so the gate holds the platform's own signed verdict (the insurance pattern). A moderator (performer) submits the determination as the completion; a reviewer/lead (principal) accepts/rejects via /verdict BEFORE the action is applied. The engine enforces, at structural validation: at least one decision type present (visibility/monetary/provision/account) and DSA conditional-required reasons (ground=illegal => legal-ground+explanation; ground=incompatible => its ground+explanation; visibility=OTHER => the free-text other). `automatedDecision` (DSA automated_detection's sibling) records whether THIS decision was made by automated means — the agent-native field; it is self-attested (notary fidelity, not prevention). GATE + SoD ARE ORCHESTRATOR-ENFORCED, NOT TYPE-ENFORCED (see README Lessons): gateMode:auto can override defaultGateMode:principal (F-791/api#838) and principal==performer self-commitment is not rejected (F-758/api#759). The orchestrator MUST route account-termination, illegal-content grounds, repeat-offender strikes, and low-confidence calls through the principal gate, MUST NOT set gateMode:auto on those, and MUST provision a moderator identity distinct from the appeal reviewer.

contentRef.description: Platform identifier of the content/account being decided. Same value as on the originating flag notice.

noticeRef.description: recordId of the content-flag-notice-v1 this decision descends from (chain lineage).

statementCategory.description: DSA STATEMENT_CATEGORY the decision is grounded in (may revise the flag's category).

contentType.description: DSA content_type of the content being acted on.

moderationConfidence.description: Optional classifier/reviewer confidence (0..1). The orchestrator's routing input: low confidence forces the human gate. Carried for audit; not engine-enforced.

territorialScope.description: Optional ISO country codes the restriction applies to (DSA territorial_scope), e.g. geo-blocking limited to certain member states.

decisionVisibility.description: DSA visibility restriction applied to the content, if any. REMOVED/DISABLED are takedowns; DEMOTED/INTERACTION_RESTRICTED/LABELLED are softer.

decisionMonetary.description: DSA monetary restriction (e.g. demonetization) applied, if any.

decisionProvision.description: DSA restriction on the provision of the service to the recipient, if any.

decisionAccount.description: DSA account-level restriction, if any. TERMINATED is the most severe enforcement and MUST go through the human gate.

decisionGround.description: DSA decision_ground: ILLEGAL_CONTENT (contrary to law) vs INCOMPATIBLE_CONTENT (contrary to the platform's terms). Selects which explanation fields are required.

automatedDecision.description: DSA automated_decision — whether THIS enforcement decision was taken by fully automated means, partially, or not at all. Self-attested: AGLedger notarizes the claim and binds it to the deciding identity; it does not verify the claim's truth.

decisionFacts.description: DSA decision_facts — the human-readable facts and circumstances the decision relied on. This is the substance of the Art.17 statement of reasons.

illegalContentLegalGround.description: DSA illegal_content_legal_ground — the legal provision relied on. Required when decisionGround=ILLEGAL_CONTENT.

illegalContentExplanation.description: DSA illegal_content_explanation — why the content is illegal. Required when decisionGround=ILLEGAL_CONTENT.

incompatibleContentGround.description: DSA incompatible_content_ground — the term/policy relied on. Required when decisionGround=INCOMPATIBLE_CONTENT.

incompatibleContentExplanation.description: DSA incompatible_content_explanation — why the content breaches the term. Required when decisionGround=INCOMPATIBLE_CONTENT.

redressMechanism.description: Optional: the redress route disclosed to the user (DSA Art.17(3)). internal_complaint is the Art.20 appeal this recipe gates.

endDateVisibilityRestriction.description: Optional: when a time-limited visibility restriction ends (DSA end_date_visibility_restriction).

endDateAccountRestriction.description: Optional: when a time-limited account restriction ends (DSA end_date_account_restriction).
